lede: A look at July 12 through the almanac — historic events, famous birthdays including Diabé Bolumbu and JP Pietersen, and the day's notable deaths and observances.

events.title: Gazpromavia Flight 9608
events.desc: Gazpromavia Flight 9608 crashes in Russia's Kolomensky District near Kolomna, killing three.

events.title: France national football team
events.desc: France win their first World Cup title, defeating defending champions Brazil 3–0.

events.title: Ulster Volunteer Force
events.desc: The Ulster Volunteer Force attacked a house in Ballymoney, County Antrim, Northern Ireland with a petrol bomb, killing the Quinn brothers.

events.title: Gorton
events.desc: Pauline Reade, 16, disappears in Gorton, England, the first victim in the Moors murders.

events.title: World War II
events.desc: World War II: Battle of Kursk: German and Soviet forces engage in the Battle of Prokhorovka, one of the largest armored engagements of all time.

events.title: Soviet–Lithuanian Peace Treaty
events.desc: The Soviet–Lithuanian Peace Treaty is signed, by which Soviet Russia recognizes the independence of Lithuania.

events.title: Second Revolution (Republic of China)
events.desc: The Second Revolution breaks out against the Beiyang government, as Li Liejun proclaims Jiangxi independent from the Republic of China.

events.title: 1847 Woodstock riot
events.desc: A riot occurred in Woodstock, New Brunswick, between Catholics and members of the Orange Order that resulted in up to ten deaths.

events.title: Jacques Necker
events.desc: In response to the dismissal of the French finance minister Jacques Necker, the radical journalist Camille Desmoulins gives a speech which results in the storming of the Bastille two days later.

events.title: Lê Cung Hoàng
events.desc: Lê Cung Hoàng ceded the throne to Mạc Đăng Dung, ending the Lê dynasty and starting the Mạc dynasty.

births.name: Ben Burtt
births.line: American director, screenwriter, and sound designer

births.name: Satoshi Ōmura
births.line: Japanese biochemist and academic, Nobel Prize laureate

deaths.text: Tonke Dragt, Dutch children's writer and illustrator
Kenneth J. Gray, American soldier and politician
Bobby Murcer, American baseball player, coach, and sportscaster
Caroline Pafford Miller, American journalist and author
Ray Stannard Baker, American journalist and author
Johann Joachim Quantz, German flute player and composer

observances.text: Christian feast day: Feast of Saints Peter and Paul (Eastern Orthodox)
Christian feast day: Jason of Thessalonica (Catholic Church)
Christian feast day: Louis Martin and Marie-Azélie Guérin
Christian feast day: Nathan Söderblom (Lutheran, Episcopal Church (USA))
